<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << username;  // Length-prefixed string
        stream << scores;    // Vector with size prefix
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata);  // Null-terminated string
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    using namespace hexi;
    
    block_allocator allocator;
    write_adaptor adaptor;
    stream_adaptor stream(allocator, adaptor);
    
    packet.serialise(stream);
    
    const auto* buffer = stream.data();
    size_t size = stream.size();
    
    return std::vector<uint8_t>(buffer, buffer + size);
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct UserData {
    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;
    std::string name;

    }

    UserData data;
    data.serialise(reader);
    return data;

    stream.read(&item_count, sizeof(item_count));

    // Check if buffer has enough data for the items
    if (buffer.size() < 5 + item_bytes) {
        return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
    }
    
    // Read the items based on data type
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
    
    if (data_type == 0) {
        int_items.resize(item_count);

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    uint32_t required_space = 4 + 4 * items.size();
    uint32_t available_space = adaptor.capacity() - adaptor.size();
    
    if (required_space > available_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    stream.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    stream.write_container(items);
    
    return true;
}
```</answer>

So for the header, which is a uint32_t, I can write its bytes directly. Then for the data vector, write the entire vector's bytes.

But wait, what if the data is empty? Well, the function parameters don't check for that, so we just proceed as per the code.

But how to handle the reading process? The dynamic_buffer might have a read method that reads a certain number of bytes into a buffer. So for the header, read sizeof(uint32_t) bytes into header_value. Then for the data, read data_length bytes into the vector.

Then for the data:

Then return a BufferData object with header_value and data.

But what if the buffer doesn't have enough data? The problem statement doesn't mention error handling, so perhaps we assume that the buffer has sufficient data. So no error checking here.

Putting it all together:

For write_to_dynamic_buffer:

- Get the header and data.
- Write header's bytes to buffer.
- Write data's bytes to buffer.

For read_from_dynamic_buffer:

- Read sizeof(uint32_t) bytes into header.
- Read data_length bytes into the data vector.
- Return the struct with these values.

Now, let's code this.

Wait, the template parameter for block_size in write_to_dynamic_buffer is optional (default 1024). So the function signature uses the same block_size as the buffer. But when writing, the buffer's block_size might affect how data is stored, but the write methods should handle that regardless.

So the code would be:

In write_to_dynamic_buffer:

buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());

In read_from_dynamic_buffer:

uint32_t header;
buffer.read(&header, sizeof(header));

std::vector<uint8_t> data(data_length);
buffer.read(data.data(), data_length);

Return {header, data};

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void write_to_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    buffer.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferData {
    uint32_t header;
    std::vector<uint8_t> data;
};

BufferData<> read_from_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<1024>& buffer, size_t data_length) {
    uint32_t header;
    buffer.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> data(data_length);
    buffer.read(data.data(), data_length);
    
    return {header, data};
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/tls_block_allocator.h>
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
using tls_allocator_type = hexi::tls_block_allocator<hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>, allocator_count>;

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void enter_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.enter_thread();
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void exit_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.exit_thread();
}
```

**Explanation:**
- `enter_thread_with_allocator` calls `allocator.enter_thread()` to initialize thread-local storage for the allocator.
- `exit_thread_with_allocator` calls `allocator.exit_thread()` to clean up thread-local resources when the thread exits.</answer>

#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <filesystem>

size_t get_file_readable_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer;

    }
    return 0;
}

bool is_file_buffer_valid(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer;
    buffer.open(file_path);
    return buffer.valid();
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_header(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t header_size) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer(file_path);
    std::vector<uint8_t> header(header_size);
    buffer.peek(header.data(), header_size);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

int32_t read_int32_from_file(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path);
    char buffer[4];
    fb.read(buffer, 4);
    return *reinterpret_cast<int32_t*>(buffer);
}

float read_float_from_file(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path);
    char buffer[4];
    fb.read(buffer, 4);
    return *reinterpret_cast<float*>(buffer);
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo<> info;
    info.capacity = buffer.capacity();
    info.current_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = buffer.capacity() - buffer.size();
    return info;
}
```</answer>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_buffer_data(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    size_t available_bytes = std::min(bytes_to_preview, buffer.size());
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(available_bytes);

}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to implement these two functions using the Hexi library. Let's start by understanding what the problem requires.

Prefixed format usually means that the length of the string is written before the actual string data. So when writing, we need to write the length first, then the string content. When reading, we read the length first, then read that many characters.

Looking at the code structure provided, the write function takes a binary_stream_writer and a string. The read function returns a string and takes a binary_stream_reader.

In C++, when dealing with binary streams, we often use write() and read() methods. But here, since it's using the Hexi library, I should check how they handle binary operations. The Hexi::pmc namespace probably has classes for binary streams.

For writing a string: The steps would be to get the size of the string, convert that size to bytes (probably as an integer), write that integer to the stream, then write the string's character data.

But wait, how does the Hexi library handle writing integers? Maybe there's a method like write_int() or write_bytes(). Alternatively, maybe we can use the buffer_adaptor to write the size as bytes.

Similarly, for reading: Read the size first, then read that many bytes into a buffer, then construct the string from that buffer.

So for write_string_to_stream:

4. Then write the actual string data using write_bytes or similar.

For read_string_from_stream:

1. Read the length first. The reader should have a read_int() method or read_bytes() to read the integer.
2. Once we have the length, read exactly that many bytes from the stream into a buffer.
3. Convert the buffer to a string.

But how exactly do these methods work in the Hexi library? Let me think. The binary_stream_writer and reader probably have methods like write_bytes() and read_bytes(), which take a buffer and a size. Also, maybe there's a way to write integers directly.

Alternatively, the buffer_adaptor might be used to adapt a buffer for reading/writing. For example, to write the size, we can create a buffer_adaptor from the size, then write it to the stream. Similarly, read the size into a buffer_adaptor, then extract the integer value.

Wait, looking at the Hexi documentation (if I had access), but since I don't, I'll proceed based on common patterns.

Assuming that binary_stream_writer has a write_bytes(const void* data, size_t size) method, and binary_stream_reader has a read_bytes(void* buffer, size_t size) method. Also, perhaps the library provides methods to write/read integers in a portable way, like write_int32() and read_int32().

So for writing the length: if the string length is stored as a 32-bit integer, we can write it as such. But the actual size of the string could be larger than 32 bits. Hmm, but in practice, strings in most systems have a reasonable maximum length, so maybe using a fixed-size integer like uint32_t for the length prefix is acceptable.

So in the write function:

Then, write the uint32_t value to the stream, followed by the string data.

For reading:

- Read a uint32_t value from the stream, which gives the length.
- Then read exactly that many bytes into a buffer.
- Create a string from the buffer.

But how to handle the buffer? The Hexi library might have a string_adaptor or something similar. Alternatively, we can use a std::vector<char> as a buffer.

Let me check the Hexi documentation. The binary_stream_writer and reader have write and read methods that can handle fundamental types. So yes, using write<uint32_t> and read<uint32_t> should work.

So putting it all together:

- Get the size of the container.
- Write the size as a uint32_t.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {
    // Write the count as a 32-bit integer
    writer.write<uint32_t>(container.size());
    // Write each element in the container
    for (uint32_t value : container) {
        writer.write<uint32_t>(value);
    }
}

std::vector<uint32_t> read_container_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    // Read the count first
    uint32_t count = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    // Create a vector of that size
    std::vector<uint32_t> container(count);
    // Read each element into the vector
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        container[i] = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    }
    return container;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return {buffer.size(), buffer.free()};
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {

    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {
        if (buffer.data()[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    const auto size = buffer.size();
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {
        if (buffer.data()[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    const auto size = buffer.size();
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {

            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

const void* get_read_pointer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return buffer.read_ptr();
}

struct BufferState {
    std::size_t size;
    const void* read_ptr;
    bool is_empty;
};

BufferState get_buffer_state(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferState state;
    state.size = buffer.size();
    state.read_ptr = buffer.read_ptr();
    state.is_empty = (buffer.size() == 0);
    return state;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string_view sv;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(sv);
    return std::string(sv);
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>

template<std::size_t Size>

    arr.fill(value);
    return arr;

#include <hexi/exception.h>

struct ExceptionDetails {

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as regular varint
    hexi::write_varint(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Serialize tags count using prefixed_varint
    hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.tags.size()));
    
    // Serialize each tag with its size using prefixed_varint
    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, static_cast<uint32_t>(tag.size()));
        buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tag.begin(), tag.end());
    }
    
    // Serialize data size using prefixed_varint
    hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.data.size()));
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    size_t offset = 0;
    CompactPacket packet;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    packet.packet_id = hexi::read_varint(buffer, offset);
    
    // Deserialize tags count using prefixed_varint
    uint32_t tags_count = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
    packet.tags.reserve(tags_count);
    
    // Deserialize each tag
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < tags_count; ++i) {
        uint32_t tag_size = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
        std::string tag(buffer.data() + offset, tag_size);
        offset += tag_size;
        packet.tags.push_back(std::move(tag));
    }
    
    // Deserialize data size using prefixed_varint
    uint32_t data_size = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
    packet.data.assign(buffer.data() + offset, data_size);
    offset += data_size;
    
    return packet;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Write header
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.header);
    
    // Write data length as uint16_t
    uint16_t data_len = static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size());
    hexi::write(buffer, data_len);
    
    // Write data string
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.data);
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    hexi::write(buffer, padding);
    
    // Write footer
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.footer);
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

    // Write data to the buffer
    write_adaptor.write(input_data.data(), input_data.size());

    // Check readable size
    auto readable_size = read_adaptor.size();
    
    // Read the data back

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct Header {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
};

Header peek_header_without_reading(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    Hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> temp(6);
    adaptor.copy(temp.data(), 6);
    
    // Verify the read pointer hasn't advanced
    const uint8_t* current_ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Construct Header from peeked data
    Header header;
    header.magic = (temp[0] << 24) | (temp[1] << 16) | (temp[2] << 8) | temp[3];
    header.version = (temp[4] << 8) | temp[5];
    
    return header;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_then_conditionally_read(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    if (buffer.empty()) return {};
    
    Hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> temp(1);
    adaptor.copy(temp.data(), 1);
    
    uint8_t first_byte = temp[0];
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    
    if (first_byte == 0x01) { // Example condition: if first byte is 0x01
        result.resize(buffer.size() - 1);
        adaptor.read(result.data(), buffer.size() - 1);
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <span>

    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto big_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(big_checksum);
    
    return stream.get_buffer();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t msg_id = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(msg_id);
    
    // Read count of data_values (converted from big-endian to native)

    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native

    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {

    // Get a span of the first 4 bytes (message_id)

    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto big_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);

}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t msg_id = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(msg_id);
    
    // Read count of data_values (converted from big-endian to native)
    uint32_t count = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count);
    
    // Read data_values vector, converting each element from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values = stream.read_container<uint16_t>(count);
    for (auto& val : data_values) {
        val = hexi::endian::big_to_native(val);
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);
    
    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t checksum = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    ProtocolHeader header;
    
    // Get a span of the first 4 bytes (message_id)
    auto header_span = buffer.first(4);
